yardstick.instrument: Clean pnpm install (--frozen-lockfile), then production build via the repo's `pnpm run build:web` (or equivalent). Measure web/dist with find/stat (apparent bytes). Parse web/dist/index.html as ground truth for initial load: every <script type="module" src=...> plus every <link rel="modulepreload" href=...> plus the entry <link rel="stylesheet">. Sum those asset byte sizes (and gzip -9 per file when reporting transfer size). Do not use single-chunk proxy or counterfactual rebuilds for cohort ranking.
yardstick.method_rationale: Vite is the build tool (web/package.json build = `tsc && vite build`). The deterministic, toolchain-native artifact is the dist/ folder and its index.html, which is the same yardstick usable for any Vite frontend. Byte sizes from the OS (stat/find/du) are ground truth rather than the team's collector script.

definitions.dist_path: web/dist (adapt if the frontend package differs)
definitions.initial_load_js: Sum of raw bytes for all JS referenced for first paint in index.html: module script(s) + modulepreload link(s). Shiv-style preloads count.
definitions.initial_load_js_css: initial_load_js plus entry stylesheet href from index.html
definitions.total_js_css: find-sum of all *.js + *.css under dist (apparent bytes, not du blocks)
definitions.total_dist_apparent: find-sum of all files under dist (apparent bytes)
definitions.total_dist_du: du -sh block size — report for cross-check only; inflates with many tiny chunks
definitions.gzip: gzip -9 -c per initial-load JS file, summed (transfer-size proxy)
definitions.baseline_pct: Do not certify reduction % without a reproducible pre-change build/commit; measure and rank after-state under pinned definitions

commands: stat -c%s on each initial-load asset; optional gzip -9 -c | wc -c per JS file
evidence_requirements: Numbers must trace to commands run in the sandbox.

revisit_if: Repo lacks pnpm build:web — adapt to equivalent production frontend build script only if replay fails
index.html uses SSR or inline scripts with no module tags — challenge with comparability argument
No pre-change baseline in clone — report after-state only; do not invent counterfactual builds

definitions.total_js_css: find-sum of all *.js + *.css under dist (apparent bytes, not du blocks)

definitions.total_dist_du: du -sh block size — report for cross-check only; inflates with many tiny chunks

yardstick.instrument: Hybrid yardstick: (1) cohort TypeScript AST counts via g4a-harness/ts_violation_counter.js on api/src,web/src,shared/src excluding tests (or repo's own AST counter if equivalent); (2) judgment protocol — sample fix patterns in production code (any→typed, as casts, non-null, as-unknown-as, Zod/typed DB boundaries); (3) pnpm type-check across packages if available. Adapt paths to repo; do not trust ESLint-only or markdown-only counts.
yardstick.method_rationale: This is a QUALITATIVE gate, not a count. The repo is a single squashed commit (baseline sha 5731a92 is absent from history), so per-line before/after diffs are not available. The strongest available evidence of meaningfulness is therefore the current code's typing patterns plus a clean strict-mode type-check (which proves any-removals were not silently re-broken). I used the toolchain's own type-checker as ground truth rather than trusting the ledger.

definitions.ast_scope: api/src, web/src, shared/src — production only (exclude tests) unless spec says otherwise
definitions.judgment_gate: Meaningful vs superficial fixes per spec; label as judgment with confidence
definitions.functionality: type-check pass is necessary not sufficient; note if tests not run

commands: grep samples: as unknown as, req.userId!, .query<, safeParse in production src
yardstick.inspection_protocol: Qualitative gate assessment of type-safety fix quality, grounded in: (1) the repo's own TypeScript AST counter (scripts/type-safety-counts.mjs) re-run at HEAD to verify remaining-violation counts vs the committed baseline (my-docs/evidence/cat1-baseline-ast-counts-5731a92.json); (2) static inspection of the actual fix patterns in current code (auth-context helper, typed pool.query<T>(), Zod safeParse boundaries, `as unknown as` double-cast prevalence); (3) running `tsc --noEmit` type-check across all three packages as the cheap functionality/correctness gate; (4) cross-referencing AUDIT_REPORT.md / IMPROVEMENT_REPORT.md / submission-ledger.json claims.
yardstick.judgment_rubric: JUDGMENT (not measurement): The type-safety fixes appear MEANINGFUL, not superficial. Evidence: (a) non-null assertions were replaced with a genuine narrowing helper getAuthenticatedRouteContext/getAuthenticatedUserContext that throws on missing req.userId/workspaceId — 0 `req.userId!`/`req.workspaceId!` remain in production routes; (b) DB boundaries are typed via pool.query<RowType>() (414 occurrences) and request bodies validated via Zod safeParse (19 route files), i.e. real narrowing rather than blanket casts; (c) the lazy `as unknown as` double-cast escape hatch appears exactly ONCE in production code (api/src/routes/claude.ts), so the 'any→unknown without narrowing' anti-pattern is NOT pervasive; (d) strict mode + noImplicitAny + noUncheckedIndexedAccess are enabled and `tsc --noEmit` passes cleanly in all three packages, so the large any reduction (94→1 prod) did not just move errors out of sight. Functionality at the type level is preserved (clean type-check). CAVEAT: I could not inspect line-by-line diffs of removed violations because history is squashed to a single commit and the baseline commit is absent; this judgment rests on current code state + type-check + documentation, not on the actual removed-line diffs, so I cannot rule out that some individual removals were cosmetic.
evidence_requirements: Label conclusions as judgment, not measurement.
State confidence and what would raise it.
Say I don't know rather than invent numbers.
Numbers must trace to commands run in the sandbox.

revisit_if: A better method would compare the exact list of removed violation sites (file:line:snippet) between baseline and HEAD and classify each as meaningful/superficial. That requires either the team's per-violation baseline dump (the committed baseline JSON only has aggregate counts) or the original commit. Revisit if either becomes available.

Blocker encountered: Repo history is a single squashed commit and the baseline commit (5731a92) is not present, so per-line before/after fix diffs cannot be inspected. Full test suite not run (requires a Postgres test database); functionality verified only via
